meta.description: Module 6 — Trade Hypothesis Builder. Structured research hypotheses assembled from Modules 1-5. NEVER instructions to trade. SAMPLE content. Update per docs/DAILY_UPDATE_GUIDE.md.
meta.methodology: A hypothesis is generated when the modules disagree — a dislocation between physical data, market structure, positioning or news. Each one is stated falsifiably: the evidence for it, the specific conditions that would invalidate it, and a confidence score. Confidence is an evidence-weight score (how much of the framework supports the hypothesis), NOT a probability of profit and NOT a signal. Instruments listed are markets to research further — never recommendations to buy or sell.

hypotheses.title: Diesel cracks are undervalued relative to refinery outage risk

evidence.point: Refinery outage alerts rising — Rotterdam has removed 400 kb/d of European crude runs, with duration uncertainty skewed longer.
Distillate stocks below seasonal average — the cushion for absorbing a supply loss is thin.
Freight into Europe tightening — clean MR rates firming as replacement barrels get pulled across the Atlantic.
The diesel crack has not repriced — still marked cheap and loosening despite the above; managed money near record short adds squeeze fuel.

invalidation: The Rotterdam refinery returns earlier than expected (restart inside two weeks).
Distillate stocks build for two consecutive weeks.
Freight into Europe normalises (clean MR rates give back the move).
hypotheses.confidence_note: Three strong and one moderate evidence leg; the offset is that diesel demand itself remains genuinely weak (the crack can stay cheap if the outage resolves quickly). Score reflects evidence weight, not probability of profit.
hypotheses.risk_note: Crowded-short squeezes are violent but brief — timing risk is high, and a fast refinery restart removes the entire thesis.

hypotheses.title: Brent's 6-month backwardation is overpriced relative to Atlantic physical length

evidence.point: West African loadings 2.3σ above the 30-day norm with unsold cargoes rerouting — physical length building in the Atlantic Basin.
The WAF→Asia freight-adjusted arb is negative — the barrels have no natural eastern home at current prices.
Brent M1/M7 marked expensive and contradicted by physical — the only spread on the board with that combination.
Managed money heavily long Brent at the 82nd percentile — the exit is crowded if the deferred story cracks.

invalidation: August WAF loading programmes are revised down materially.
Chinese buying resumes and clears the overhang (discharge activity re-accelerates).
Floating storage draws for two consecutive weeks instead of building.
hypotheses.confidence_note: The prompt tightness is real (stock draws), so this is strictly a deferred-structure hypothesis — the front of the curve is not in question. Evidence-weight score only.
hypotheses.risk_note: OPEC+ rhetoric can steepen backwardation abruptly regardless of Atlantic fundamentals — event risk cuts both ways.

hypotheses.title: Compliant tanker earnings strength is structural, not a spot blip

evidence.point: TD3C firm at +1.2σ with flat cargo counts — the move is fleet-supply driven, which persists; demand blips don't.
New shadow-fleet designations shrink the compliant fleet further and lengthen average voyages.
Floating storage building (+6 mb m/m) ties up additional tonnage.

invalidation: Sanctions enforcement visibly relaxes (designated vessels resume normal trading).
Floating storage unwinds quickly, releasing tonnage.
Newbuild ordering accelerates materially at the next round of yard slots.
hypotheses.confidence_note: Directionally supported but every leg is second-order — hence monitoring status rather than active. Evidence-weight score only.
instruments_to_research: Tanker owner equities (see Investment Watchlist — monitored names, not recommendations)
Baltic Exchange dirty tanker indices (licensed data)
hypotheses.risk_note: Freight is among the most volatile series in the complex; position sizing research matters more than direction here.
